wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ZB-DEZINE project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a multi-faceted Flutter-based e-commerce and AI design platform, its core value proposition, and target audience. Document the technology stack including Flutter SDK, GetX state management, and Material Design 3. Describe the application's key features including AI-powered design tools, e-commerce functionality, user management, and business operations. Include architectural overview showing the modular MVVM pattern with GetX controllers. Provide context about the project's scope and positioning in the market. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of the platform's capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's core infrastructure components. Detail the dependency injection system using GetX, explaining service registration patterns and lifecycle management. Document the routing architecture with type-safe navigation and authentication-aware routes. Explain the theme management system including Material Design 3 implementation, light/dark mode support, and color system. Cover local storage mechanisms for tokens and preferences. Include practical examples of service initialization, route configuration, and theme switching. Address service dependencies, singleton patterns, and global state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the ZB-DEZINE Flutter project. Include step-by-step installation instructions for Flutter SDK, required development tools (Android Studio/VS Code), and project setup procedures. Document prerequisite knowledge including Flutter fundamentals, Dart programming basics, and mobile app development concepts. Provide clear instructions for running the application on different platforms (Android, iOS, web). Include common setup issues and troubleshooting solutions. Demonstrate how to run the app locally, configure environment variables, and verify successful installation. Make content accessible to developers new to Flutter while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get up and running.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the user authentication flow implementation. Document the complete login and registration process including form validation, API integration, and response handling. Explain the SigninController and SignupController implementations, their methods, and state management. Detail the authentication repository patterns used for login and registration operations. Include code examples showing form submission, error handling, and successful authentication flows. Document the integration with StorageService for token persistence and navigation to protected routes. Address common authentication scenarios, validation rules, and security considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bottom Navigation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the bottom navigation system in ZB-DEZINE's user dashboard. Detail the BottomNavController implementation including page management, selected index tracking, and navigation state handling. Document the five-tab navigation structure covering Home, Categories, Dashboard, and Profile views. Explain the reactive state management using GetX controllers and observable variables. Include examples of adding new navigation tabs, customizing tab appearance, and handling navigation events. Address the navigation lifecycle, page caching strategies, and performance considerations for smooth tab switching.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Design Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AI design generation functionality. Document the design creation workflow, including category selection (AI Product Placement, AI Interior Design), search functionality, and pagination controls. Explain the controller implementation for managing design states, filtering mechanisms, and data binding patterns. Detail the UI components for design display, including expandable rows, status filtering, and interactive elements. Document the design model structure, data flow from controller to view, and state management using GetX reactive programming. Include examples of design generation processes, category-based filtering, and user interaction patterns. Address performance considerations for large design lists and memory management strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Catalog Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the product catalog management system. Document the category organization structure, product listing mechanisms, and browsing interfaces. Explain the implementation of product filtering, sorting, and search functionality. Detail the product data models, inventory management integration, and pricing systems. Include examples of category hierarchies, product categorization logic, and catalog navigation patterns. Document the relationship between categories and products, product attributes management, and catalog performance optimization. Address product discovery algorithms, recommendation systems, and user engagement features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rental System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the rental system functionality. Document the complete furniture rental workflow including rental request creation, approval processes, and rental lifecycle management. Explain the RentalController implementation with pagination, filtering by status, and search functionality. Detail the RentalsModel structure and data flow from repository to UI. Document the rent request controller for managing rental applications, quoting, and approval workflows. Include examples of rental status management (Active, Pending, Quoted, Completed), pagination handling, and debounced search implementation. Address business rules for rental terms, pricing calculations, and inventory tracking integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive dashboard analytics documentation for ZB-DEZINE's administrative interface. Document the business insights and performance metrics dashboard including sales analytics, revenue tracking, and user engagement metrics. Explain the data visualization components, chart implementations, and KPI displays. Detail the recent orders table functionality, expandable rows, and order management features. Document the quick action buttons system for shop products, sell furniture, rent products, and design services. Include examples of dashboard layouts, metric calculations, and real-time data updates. Address dashboard customization options, filter mechanisms, and export capabilities for business reporting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's data models and schemas. Document the error model structure including error codes, messages, and handling patterns. Detail the user profile model with all fields, validation rules, and serialization methods. Explain the data model inheritance patterns, field types, and relationships between models. Include examples of model instantiation, JSON serialization/deserialization, and validation workflows. Document the model lifecycle, immutability patterns, and best practices for data manipulation. Address model versioning, backward compatibility, and migration strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive network services documentation for ZB-DEZINE's HTTP client implementation. Document the GetNetwork class and its generic getData method for handling GET requests with proper error handling and response parsing. Explain the post_with_response.dart implementation for POST requests with payload handling and status code management. Detail the delete_network.dart for DELETE operations and HTTP status code handling. Include the networks_path.dart constants for API endpoint management. Provide examples of API integration patterns, request/response handling, error management strategies, and authentication header implementation. Document the Either type usage for functional error handling, JSON parsing patterns, and network timeout configurations. Address security considerations, SSL certificate handling, and network connectivity monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's network services layer. Detail the HTTP networking implementation including GET, POST, and DELETE operations with standardized request/response handling patterns. Document the GetNetwork class for data retrieval operations, PostWithResponse and PostWithoutResponse classes for API interactions, and DeleteNetwork for resource deletion. Explain the functional programming approach using Either type for error handling, including success and failure scenarios. Cover base URL configuration, header management, status code handling, and error model integration. Include practical examples of making API calls, handling different response types, and implementing retry mechanisms. Address timeout configurations, authentication headers, and network error recovery strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dependency Injection System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's dependency injection system built on GetX. Detail the service registration patterns in the DependencyInjection class, explaining how services are initialized during app startup. Document the service lifecycle management with permanent service registration using Get.put(). Explain the service dependencies including StorageService, ThemeService, ThemeController, and network services. Cover the initialization sequence and how services are retrieved using Get.find(). Include practical examples of adding new services, managing service lifecycles, and handling service dependencies. Address singleton patterns, service initialization order, and integration with GetX's reactive state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: MVVM Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's MVVM pattern implementation using GetX. Explain how controllers manage business logic and reactive state, the binding mechanism between views and controllers, and state synchronization patterns. Document the observer pattern implementation, reactive updates, and dependency injection through GetX. Include examples of controller lifecycle management, state persistence, and error handling in controllers. Address best practices for controller organization, memory management, and performance optimization in the MVVM architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for ZB-DEZINE development. Include detailed prerequisites covering Flutter SDK installation, Dart SDK requirements, and development environment setup for Android Studio/VS Code. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including dependency installation via pubspec.yaml, running the application locally, and configuring platform-specific settings for Android and iOS. Document development workflow including hot reload, debugging setup, and testing procedures. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and environment configuration problems. Provide quick start examples for running the app on emulators and physical devices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's data layer architecture. Explain the repository pattern implementation with base repository classes and network repository abstractions. Document HTTP networking layer including GET, POST, and DELETE operations with standardized request/response handling. Detail error management strategies, retry mechanisms, and timeout configurations. Cover data serialization/deserialization patterns and API response mapping. Include examples of network service usage, repository method implementations, and error handling workflows. Address caching strategies, offline support, and data consistency patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for ZB-DEZINE. Document the core technologies including Flutter SDK (^3.9.0), Dart programming language, and Material Design 3 framework. Explain key dependencies such as GetX (^4.7.3) for state management and dependency injection, http (^1.6.0) for networking, get_storage (^2.1.1) for local persistence, and utility packages like flutter_screenutil (^5.9.3) and google_fonts (^8.0.0). Describe the development tools, IDE configuration, and code quality standards defined in analysis_options.yaml. Include version compatibility requirements, upgrade paths, and rationale for technology choices. Provide guidance on maintaining and updating the technology stack.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Verification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the email verification system. Detail the email verification controller implementation, verification code handling, and email confirmation processes. Document the email verification repository methods, API integration patterns, and verification status management. Explain the verification flow from initial email sending through code entry to account activation. Include examples of verification code validation, resend functionality, and error handling for invalid codes. Address security measures, verification timeout handling, and user experience considerations during the verification process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the profile management system in ZB-DEZINE's user dashboard. Document the ProfileController functionality including user information retrieval, update operations, and validation handling. Explain the profile views implementation covering user details display, edit forms, and address management. Detail the UserAddressModel structure and address book functionality. Include examples of profile customization, address addition/editing, and preference settings management. Document the integration between controllers and views, form validation patterns, and error handling strategies. Address security considerations for sensitive user data and privacy settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Design Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AI design management capabilities. Detail the design details controller implementation, including design preview functionality, editing options, and management operations. Document the design details view components, zoom controls, download mechanisms, and sharing features. Explain the integration between design listing and detailed views, navigation patterns, and state preservation during user interactions. Include examples of design manipulation workflows, preview customization options, and export functionality. Address design data synchronization, offline viewing capabilities, and performance optimization for image rendering. Document error handling for corrupted designs and service connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shopping Cart System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the shopping cart functionality. Detail the cart controller implementation, item management, and state persistence. Explain cart item operations including add, remove, update quantities, and cart calculations. Document the cart data models, item serialization, and local storage integration. Include examples of cart validation, inventory checking, and price calculations. Address cart session management, user cart synchronization, and cart cleanup processes. Document integration with product catalogs, pricing systems, and user authentication. Provide guidance on cart performance optimization and memory management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the transaction management system. Detail the transaction controller implementation for processing payments, tracking financial activities, and maintaining transaction history. Document the transaction model structure including payment types, amounts, statuses, and metadata. Explain repository patterns for transaction data access and persistence. Include examples of transaction workflows, payment processing integration, and financial reporting capabilities. Address transaction categorization, audit trails, and compliance requirements. Document error handling for failed transactions and reconciliation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Support System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed support system documentation for ZB-DEZINE's customer service and help desk functionality. Document the ticketing system implementation, support request submission, and resolution workflows. Explain the help desk interface, agent management, and customer communication channels. Detail the support categories, priority handling, and escalation procedures. Document the ticket lifecycle from creation to closure, including status tracking and response times. Include examples of support interactions, FAQ management, and knowledge base integration. Address support analytics, SLA monitoring, and customer satisfaction metrics. Cover integration with external support tools and automated response systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's shared widget library and utility components. Document reusable UI components including buttons, input fields, cards, and layout containers. Explain component props, events, and customization options. Detail the utility functions for data formatting, validation, and transformation. Document helper extensions for common operations and string manipulations. Include usage examples with code snippets showing component integration patterns. Address component composition, styling consistency, and responsive design patterns. Document performance optimization techniques and accessibility considerations for shared components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Storage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed local storage documentation for ZB-DEZINE's data persistence layer. Document the StorageService class implementation for token management, user preferences, and application state persistence. Explain the theme_service.dart for theme preference handling and dark/light mode persistence. Detail the get_storage package integration, key-value pair management, and data serialization strategies. Include examples of secure token storage, preference retrieval patterns, and data migration handling. Document the storage lifecycle management, cleanup procedures, and data integrity validation. Address security considerations for sensitive data, encryption strategies, and backup/restore mechanisms. Explain the relationship between local storage and application state management, offline-first data handling, and cache invalidation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Repository Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's repository pattern implementation. Explain the BaseRepository abstract class design and its role in providing common data access functionality across all repositories. Document the NetworkRepository implementation showing how it extends base functionality with network-specific operations. Detail the repository abstraction layer benefits including testability, maintainability, and separation of concerns. Cover method signatures, parameter handling, and return value patterns. Include examples of implementing custom repositories, handling asynchronous operations, and integrating with the dependency injection system. Address error propagation, data transformation, and caching strategies within repositories. Explain how repositories interact with network services and provide clean interfaces to controllers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's routing architecture using GetX navigation. Explain the route definition patterns in app_routes.dart and routes.dart, documenting type-safe navigation and route parameters. Detail the authentication-aware routing system that controls access to protected routes. Cover route guards, navigation state management, and deep linking support. Include practical examples of programmatic navigation, route parameter passing, and conditional navigation based on user authentication status. Document the relationship between route definitions and view controllers, explaining how routes map to specific screens and controllers. Address navigation stack management, route transitions, and integration with the overall app architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Widget Composition and Reusability
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's widget composition patterns and reusable component architecture. Detail the shared widgets library structure, component composition strategies, and reusability patterns. Document custom widget development guidelines, parameter passing mechanisms, and event handling patterns. Include examples of stateful and stateless widget composition, conditional rendering, and dynamic widget building. Address widget performance optimization, memory management, and testing strategies for reusable components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for ZB-DEZINE's system design. Describe the high-level modular MVVM architecture with GetX controllers, dependency injection patterns, and repository pattern implementation. Document component interactions, data flows between layers, and integration patterns. Explain technical decisions including reactive state management with GetX, modular routing system, and theme management. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing core modules, feature boundaries, and data flow patterns. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, caching strategies, and performance optimization. Document technology stack, third-party dependencies, and version compatibility matrix.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Architecture and Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's UI architecture and design patterns. Explain the MVVM pattern implementation with GetX controllers managing reactive state. Document widget composition patterns, reusable component architecture, and screen organization. Detail the bottom navigation system, screen transitions, and responsive design considerations. Cover custom widget development, theme integration, and Material Design 3 implementation. Include examples of controller-view binding, state management patterns, and UI state synchronization. Address performance optimization techniques, memory management, and UI testing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for ZB-DEZINE's modular MVVM architecture with GetX. Describe the high-level design patterns including Model-View-ViewModel architecture, dependency injection using GetX, and repository pattern implementation. Document the directory structure showing separation of concerns between core infrastructure, feature modules, and shared components. Explain the routing system, theme management, and state management patterns. Include architectural diagrams illustrating component relationships, data flow, and module boundaries. Address design principles, scalability considerations, and maintainability benefits. Document the modular approach that enables feature isolation and independent development.

wiki_catalogs.name: OTP Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the OTP (One-Time Password) authentication system. Document the OTP controller implementation, SMS/email OTP handling, and verification processes. Explain OTP generation, delivery mechanisms, and validation workflows. Detail the OTP repository methods for OTP requests, resend operations, and verification checks. Include code examples showing OTP input handling, timer-based expiration, and retry mechanisms. Document error handling for invalid OTP codes, rate limiting, and security measures. Address user experience considerations including OTP auto-fill, resend functionality, and timeout management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Home Content Discovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the home content discovery system in ZB-DEZINE's user dashboard. Document the HomeView implementation including featured products, personalized recommendations, trending items, and user-specific content. Explain the home controller's role in content aggregation, recommendation algorithms, and dynamic content loading. Detail the content organization strategy, carousel components, and interactive elements. Include examples of customizing homepage content, implementing content filters, and managing content freshness. Document the integration with AI design features, recent activity feeds, and promotional content management. Address performance optimization for content loading and caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Design History
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the AI design history system implementation and management. Explain the design listing functionality, including pagination controls, sorting mechanisms, and filtering by design type. Detail the design model structure with fields for ID, type, generation date, and status tracking. Document the reactive state management for design collections, expandable row functionality, and interactive design cards. Include examples of design history navigation, bulk operations, and archive management. Address data persistence strategies for design metadata, caching mechanisms for frequently accessed designs, and performance optimization for large history lists. Document the integration with cloud storage for design assets and local caching for offline access.

wiki_catalogs.name: Checkout Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the checkout workflow and order processing system. Document the checkout controller implementation, step-by-step checkout flow, and order validation. Explain address management, payment method handling, and order confirmation processes. Detail the integration with payment gateways, shipping calculations, and tax processing. Include examples of checkout form validation, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms. Document order creation, payment processing, and order status management. Address security considerations, PCI compliance, and payment data protection. Provide guidance on checkout optimization, conversion rate improvement, and customer experience enhancement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Credit Balance System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the credit balance management system. Document the credit balance controller for tracking user credits, processing credit adjustments, and maintaining credit history. Explain the credit balance model including credit limits, available balances, and transactional credit movements. Detail the repository implementation for credit data persistence and retrieval. Include examples of credit top-up workflows, deduction processes, and credit expiration handling. Address credit validation rules, overdraft protection, and credit utilization reporting. Document integration with payment systems and transaction processing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's theme management system implementing Material Design 3. Detail the ThemeController implementation for managing light/dark mode switching and theme state. Document the theme service for persisting user theme preferences and retrieving theme configurations. Explain the app_theme.dart structure including color schemes, typography scales, and design tokens. Cover the reactive theme switching mechanism using GetX state management. Include practical examples of theme customization, dynamic theme updates, and theme persistence across app sessions. Document the integration between theme controller, theme service, and UI components. Address theme consistency, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization for theme switching.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation and Screen Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's navigation system and screen management architecture. Explain the bottom navigation implementation, route definitions, and navigation state management. Document screen transitions, navigation guards, and authentication-aware routing. Include examples of programmatic navigation, deep linking, and navigation state persistence. Address navigation performance optimization, memory management during navigation, and best practices for complex navigation flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for ZB-DEZINE. Document the complete user authentication flow including onboarding, email verification, OTP validation, and user mode selection. Explain session management, token handling, and security measures. Detail the implementation of login and registration controllers, email verification processes, and OTP validation mechanisms. Document user role management and profile creation workflows. Include error handling strategies, security best practices, and integration with backend APIs. Provide code examples demonstrating authentication state management and navigation guards. Address common authentication scenarios and troubleshooting procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Modular Design and Feature Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's modular architecture and feature organization. Explain the separation of concerns between core, features, and shared modules. Document the feature-based organization with clear boundaries and dependencies. Detail the shared components library and reusable utilities. Cover module import patterns, dependency management, and inter-module communication. Include examples of adding new features, creating shared components, and maintaining module isolation. Address testing strategies for modular architecture, hot reload considerations, and build optimization for large codebases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Highlights
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive feature highlights documentation showcasing ZB-DEZINE's core capabilities. Document the multi-faceted platform including AI-powered interior design tools, e-commerce marketplace functionality, user authentication and management, rental system, transaction management, and administrative features. Explain each major feature's purpose, target users, and key benefits. Include screenshots or mockups where possible to illustrate the platform's capabilities. Describe how features integrate with each other and contribute to the overall user experience. Highlight unique selling points and competitive advantages. Provide context about the platform's positioning in the home furnishing and design market.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Modes and Profiles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for user modes and profile management system. Document the UserModeController implementation for handling different user types (customer, seller, admin). Explain profile creation workflows, user mode selection processes, and profile data management. Detail the ProfileController methods for profile updates, address management, and user preferences. Include examples of user mode switching, profile completion flows, and data validation. Document the integration between user modes and system permissions, profile data structures, and address management functionality. Address profile completeness requirements and user onboarding optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the order management system. Detail order lifecycle management, order status tracking, and order history functionality. Explain order data models, order item management, and order state transitions. Document the implementation of order retrieval, filtering, and pagination. Include examples of order status updates, customer notifications, and order modification processes. Address order analytics, sales reporting, and order fulfillment integration. Document integration with inventory systems, shipping providers, and payment processors. Provide guidance on order processing optimization, error handling, and customer service workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theming and Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for ZB-DEZINE's theming system and design tokens. Detail Material Design 3 implementation, light/dark theme switching, and color scheme management. Document typography systems, spacing scales, and design token usage throughout the application. Include examples of theme customization, dynamic theming, and responsive design considerations. Address theme performance optimization, accessibility compliance, and cross-platform design consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed AI design system documentation for ZB-DEZINE's intelligent design generation capabilities. Document the AI design generation workflow, product placement functionality, and design preview/editing features. Explain the integration with AI services, design data models, and user interaction patterns. Detail the controller implementations for design creation, editing, and management. Document the design history system, saving mechanisms, and sharing capabilities. Include API integration patterns, error handling for AI service failures, and performance optimization strategies. Provide examples of design generation processes and customization options. Address troubleshooting common AI-related issues and service integration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed development environment setup documentation for ZB-DEZINE. Document platform-specific requirements for Android (Gradle, Android SDK), iOS (Xcode, CocoaPods), web (Chrome/Chromium), Linux, macOS, and Windows builds. Explain Flutter SDK configuration, environment variables, and build tools setup. Include instructions for configuring development certificates, provisioning profiles, and signing requirements. Document IDE setup recommendations, debugging configuration, and hot reload capabilities. Cover continuous integration setup, automated testing configuration, and deployment preparation. Address common development environment issues and their solutions. Provide guidance on setting up multiple development environments for team collaboration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for session management and token handling. Document the StorageService implementation for secure token storage, session persistence, and automatic logout functionality. Explain token lifecycle management, refresh mechanisms, and security measures. Detail the integration with routing system for authentication-aware navigation and route protection. Include examples of session validation, automatic logout on token expiration, and secure token handling patterns. Document the relationship between authentication state and application navigation, including protected route access and authentication guards. Address security best practices for token storage, session timeout handling, and logout procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: E-commerce Platform
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive e-commerce platform documentation for ZB-DEZINE's shopping functionality. Document the product catalog management system, category organization, and product browsing/search capabilities. Explain the shopping cart functionality, checkout process, and payment integration. Detail the implementation of product details views, inventory management, and pricing systems. Document order processing workflows, user purchase history, and transaction management. Include integration patterns with payment gateways, shipping services, and inventory systems. Provide examples of product filtering, sorting, and recommendation algorithms. Address common e-commerce scenarios, error handling, and performance optimization for large catalogs.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed user dashboard documentation for ZB-DEZINE's personalization and navigation system. Document the bottom navigation system, quick access features, and content organization. Explain the profile management system including user information, address book functionality, and preference settings. Detail the home screen layout, content discovery features, and personalized recommendations. Document the integration between navigation controllers and view management. Include examples of customizing dashboard layouts, managing user preferences, and implementing personalized content. Address responsive design considerations and accessibility features for the dashboard interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive business operations documentation for ZB-DEZINE's commercial functionality. Document the furniture rental system including request management, rental lifecycle, and inventory tracking. Explain the transaction management system, credit balance tracking, and financial transaction history. Detail the implementation of rental controllers, request processing, and payment workflows. Document business analytics, reporting capabilities, and operational metrics. Include integration patterns with accounting systems, payment processors, and inventory management. Provide examples of rental workflows, transaction processing, and financial reporting. Address business rule enforcement, audit trails, and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed administrative features documentation for ZB-DEZINE's management and support systems. Document the dashboard analytics system including business insights, performance metrics, and data visualization. Explain the customer support system, help desk functionality, and ticketing mechanisms. Detail the admin panel implementation, user management capabilities, and content moderation tools. Document reporting systems, analytics dashboards, and business intelligence features. Include examples of administrative workflows, user management procedures, and support ticket resolution. Address security considerations for admin access, audit logging, and permission management systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data layer architecture documentation for ZB-DEZINE's data management systems. Document the network services including HTTP request handling, API communication patterns, and error management strategies. Explain the local storage implementation with token management, preference handling, and data persistence strategies. Detail the repository pattern implementation, data caching mechanisms, and offline-first approaches. Document the integration between network and local storage layers, data synchronization strategies, and conflict resolution. Include examples of API integration, data modeling, and error handling patterns. Address performance optimization, data security, and privacy considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Global Models and Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ZB-DEZINE's global models and utility components. Document the common data structures including error models, user profile models, and API response schemas. Explain the shared widget library, helper functions, and utility extensions used across the application. Detail the design system implementation, reusable component patterns, and consistency guidelines. Document the utility functions for data transformation, formatting, and validation. Include examples of model usage, component integration, and utility function applications. Address code reusability patterns, performance considerations, and maintenance strategies for shared components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for ZB-DEZINE development. Document the unit testing approach using Flutter's testing framework, widget testing implementation, and mock data strategies. Explain the testing patterns for GetX controllers, repository classes, and service implementations. Detail the testing setup, test configuration, and continuous integration considerations. Document testing best practices for state management, asynchronous operations, and API integration testing. Include examples of test implementation, mocking strategies, and test automation. wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Build Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and build configuration documentation for ZB-DEZINE across all supported platforms. Document platform-specific build configurations for Android, iOS, Web, Linux, macOS, and Windows. Explain the release configuration process, code signing requirements, and distribution strategies. Detail the CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and deployment automation. Document platform-specific optimizations, asset bundling, and performance tuning. Include examples of build scripts, release workflows, and troubleshooting deployment issues. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ZB-DEZINE: Flutter Multi-Feature E-commerce & AI Design Platform

## Project Introduction

**ZB-DEZINE** is a comprehensive Flutter-based multi-feature e-commerce and AI design platform designed to revolutionize interior design and furniture shopping experiences. The application combines traditional e-commerce functionality with cutting-edge AI-powered design tools, creating a unique marketplace for interior designers, homeowners, and businesses.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as an all-in-one solution for discovering, purchasing, and creating customized interior design solutions through AI-powered design generation and a comprehensive marketplace for home furnishings and decor items.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **AI Integration**: Provide advanced AI-powered design generation tools for product placement and interior design visualization
- **E-commerce Excellence**: Offer a seamless shopping experience for home furnishings and decor products
- **User Experience**: Deliver intuitive navigation between onboarding, authentication, and core platform features
- **Scalability**: Build a modular architecture supporting multiple business verticals including rentals, sales, and transactions

### Target Audience
- Interior designers seeking clients and showcasing portfolios
- Homeowners looking for personalized interior design solutions
- Furniture retailers and suppliers expanding their digital presence
- AI enthusiasts interested in creative design applications

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern Flutter architecture pattern with clear separation of concerns and dependency injection principles.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Entry Point] --> B[Dependency Injection]
    B --> C[Theme Controller]
    B --> D[Storage Service]
    B --> E[Network Services]
    
    F[Routing System] --> G[Authentication Routes]
    F --> H[Home Navigation]
    F --> I[Feature Modules]
    
    J[Feature Modules] --> K[AI Design]
    J --> L[Profile Management]
    J --> M[Dashboard]
    J --> N[Transactions]
    
    O[UI Layer] --> P[GetX Controllers]
    O --> Q[Widgets]
    O --> R[Views]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture is structured around several key layers:

**Presentation Layer**: Utilizes GetX for reactive state management and navigation
**Domain Layer**: Contains business logic and feature-specific controllers
**Data Layer**: Manages network requests, local storage, and data persistence
**Infrastructure Layer**: Handles dependency injection, theming, and configuration

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **MVVM Pattern**: Model-View-ViewModel architecture for clean separation
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracts data access logic for better testability
- **Dependency Injection**: Centralized service management using GetX
- **Observer Pattern**: Reactive state management with RxJS-inspired observables

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Authentication] --> B[Token Validation]
    B --> C{Has Token?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Home Navigation]
    C -->|No| E[Onboarding Flow]
    
    D --> F[Bottom Navigation]
    F --> G[AI Design Module]
    F --> H[Marketplace]
    F --> I[Profile Management]
    F --> J[Transactions]
    
    G --> K[AI Generation Engine]
    H --> L[E-commerce Backend]
    I --> M[User Data Management]
    J --> N[Payment Processing]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application bootstraps through a centralized entry point that initializes dependencies and determines the user's authentication state:

**Main Application Entry**
- File: `lib/main.dart`
- Initializes screen utilities, dependency injection, and theme management
- Handles conditional routing based on authentication status

**Dependency Injection System**
- File: `lib/core/di/dependency_injection.dart`
- Centralized service registration with GetX dependency injection
- Manages storage, theme, and network service lifecycle

### Core Modules

**Routing System**
- File: `lib/core/routes/app_routes.dart`
- Defines all application routes with constants for type safety
- Supports both authenticated and unauthenticated navigation flows

**Theme Management**
- File: `lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart`
- Implements Material Design 3 with custom color schemes
- Provides both light and dark theme variants

**Color System**
- File: `lib/core/constant/colors.dart`
- Comprehensive color palette with gradient definitions
- Supports both light and dark mode color variations

### Configuration Approach

**Project Configuration**
- File: `pubspec.yaml`
- Flutter SDK 3.9.0+ requirement
- Extensive dependency management for UI, networking, and business features

**Asset Management**
- Images and icons organized under `assets/` directory
- Custom fonts and icon configurations defined in Flutter section

### External Dependencies

The project leverages a comprehensive set of Flutter packages:

**UI & Styling**
- `get`: State management and dependency injection
- `flutter_screenutil`: Responsive design support
- `google_fonts`: Typography enhancements
- `glassmorphism`: Modern UI effects

**Networking & Data**
- `http`: RESTful API communication
- `get_storage`: Local data persistence
- `cached_network_image`: Optimized image loading

**Business Features**
- `intl_phone_field`: International phone number handling
- `fl_chart`: Data visualization components
- `table_calendar`: Calendar integration for scheduling

### Integration Points

**Network Layer**
- Files: `lib/core/data/networks/`
- Standardized HTTP request handling
- Consistent error response management

**Local Storage**
- Files: `lib/core/data/local/`
- Secure token storage and theme preferences
- Cross-platform data persistence

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[SigninController] --> B[LoginRepository]
    B --> C[Network Layer]
    C --> D[API Endpoints]
    
    E[ProfileController] --> F[GetProfileRepository]
    F --> G[User Data Service]
    
    H[AiDesignController] --> I[AI Generation Service]
    I --> J[Third-party AI APIs]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Faceted Platform Architecture**
- **Authentication System**: Complete user onboarding with email verification and OTP validation
- **AI Design Tools**: Advanced AI-powered design generation for product placement and interior styling
- **E-commerce Marketplace**: Comprehensive shopping experience with categories, product listings, and secure checkout
- **User Profiles**: Personalized dashboards with order history, preferences, and account management
- **Transaction Management**: Credit balance tracking, payment processing, and financial transaction history

### Implementation Highlights

**AI Design Module**
- File: `lib/features/ai_design/controller/ai_design_controller.dart`
- Advanced filtering and pagination for AI-generated designs
- Interactive design preview and customization capabilities

**User Authentication Flow**
- File: `lib/features/auth/controller/signin_controller.dart`
- Form validation with real-time feedback
- Seamless transition between authentication states

**Home Dashboard**
- File: `lib/features/home/views/home_view.dart`
- Modular component architecture for different home sections
- Dynamic content organization with quick action buttons

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Onboarding
    Onboarding --> Authentication: Complete Onboarding
    Authentication --> HomeNavigation: Valid Credentials
    Authentication --> Onboarding: Invalid Credentials
    
    HomeNavigation --> AIDesign: AI Design Tab
    HomeNavigation --> Marketplace: Shop Tab
    HomeNavigation --> Profile: Profile Tab
    HomeNavigation --> Transactions: Transactions Tab
    
    AIDesign --> DesignGeneration: Generate Designs
    Marketplace --> ProductSelection: Browse Products
    Profile --> AccountManagement: Manage Profile
    Transactions --> PaymentProcessing: Process Payments
    
    DesignGeneration --> [*]: Return to Home
    ProductSelection --> [*]: Return to Home
    AccountManagement --> [*]: Return to Home
    PaymentProcessing --> [*]: Return to Home
```

**Performance Considerations**
- Responsive design implementation using Flutter ScreenUtil
- Efficient image loading with cached_network_image
- Optimized state management with GetX reactive controllers
- Modular architecture preventing unnecessary widget rebuilds

**Security Implementation**
- Token-based authentication with automatic refresh
- Secure local storage using get_storage
- Input validation and sanitization throughout forms
- Encrypted sensitive data handling

**Extensibility Features**
- Plugin-based architecture allowing easy addition of new features
- Modular routing system supporting dynamic feature loading
- Comprehensive testing infrastructure ready for unit and widget tests

---

**Sources:**
- [pubspec.yaml](pubspec.yaml)
- [main.dart](lib/main.dart)
- [dependency_injection.dart](lib/core/di/dependency_injection.dart)
- [app_routes.dart](lib/core/routes/app_routes.dart)
- [app_theme.dart](lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart)
- [colors.dart](lib/core/constant/colors.dart)
- [signin_controller.dart](lib/features/auth/controller/signin_controller.dart)
- [home_view.dart](lib/features/home/views/home_view.dart)
- [onboarding_view.dart](lib/features/auth/views/onboarding_view.dart)
- [ai_design_controller.dart](lib/features/ai_design/controller/ai_design_controller.dart)
- [profile_controller.dart](lib/features/profile/controllers/profile_controller.dart)
- [README.md](README.md)

</blog>
